name: stride-security-review
description: AI-powered security review for code changes via the /stride-security-review:security-review slash command. Two scan modes (diff / --full), multi-ecosystem framework rule packs (Django, Phoenix, Rails — each substantially expanded in 2.2.0 with open-redirect, deserialization, SSRF, DRF mass-assignment, settings hardening, Phoenix.Token replay, System.cmd shell injection, LiveView upload guards, connection.execute injection, missing-auth, JSON-leak), multi-platform CI/CD rule pack, Web defense-in-depth pack covering security headers (CSP/HSTS/X-Frame-Options/cookie flags), MAESTRO 7-layer agentic-AI classification, RCI critique loop, baseline suppression, surgical-fix patches, SARIF v2.1.0 output for GitHub Code Scanning, --fail-on severity gating for CI, and --base <ref> for PR-against-base diffs. Ships a reference GitHub Actions workflow, a TAP 13 eval runner over 40+ fixtures with positive-and-negative-control coverage, and explicit MAESTRO mapping for non-AI framework findings. Loosely based on anthropics/claude-code-security-review.
